reforms.description: The government plans to complement R&D tax credits with targeted grants subject to strict evaluation, enhancing knowledge transfer from research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing additional funding and support for R&D, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation and improve collaboration between research institutions and the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to complement R&D tax credits with targeted grants.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing R&D support mechanisms, aiming to improve the effectiveness of public funding for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Complement R&D tax credits with targeted grants subject to strict evaluation and enhance knowledge transfer from research institutions.

reforms.description: The government aims to provide seamless support to innovative digital start-ups for their early global expansion through better coordination between Callaghan Innovation and New Zealand Trade Enterprise.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the global expansion of start-ups is expected to enhance their market reach and contribute to economic growth through innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Provide seamless support to innovative digital start-ups for their early global expansion.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards enhancing support for start-ups, which are crucial for driving innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Provide seamless support to innovative digital start-ups for their early global expansion through better coordination between Callaghan Innovation and New Zealand Trade Enterprise.

reforms.description: New Zealand plans to rigorously implement its new national digitalisation strategy to enhance digital transformation across government agencies and social partners.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to boost productivity and improve service delivery through better use of digital technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to rigorously implement the new national digitalisation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to enhance digital capabilities, which is crucial for productivity growth.
reforms.source_quote: New Zealand should rigorously implement its new national digitalisation strategy so that government agencies and social partners can advance digital transformation.

reforms.description: New Zealand launched the Elevate NZ Venture Fund to co-invest with venture capital funds in Series A and B funding, aiming to increase early-stage funding for digital innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to early-stage funding, which is crucial for the commercialization of innovative digital projects.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched the Elevate NZ Venture Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: Addresses a significant gap in early-stage funding for digital innovation, which is essential for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The lack of sufficient early-stage funding is holding back the commercialisation of digital innovation.

reforms.description: The Digital Boost Initiative aims to enhance the capacity of small businesses by providing technical and managerial advice to improve their digital capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the adoption of digital technologies among small businesses, which can lead to productivity improvements and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Boost the capacity of the Digital Boost Initiative to provide both technical and managerial advice to small businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significantly enhances support for small businesses in digital transformation, which is critical for their competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Boost the capacity of the Digital Boost Initiative to provide both technical support to small businesses.

reforms.description: The government is developing a national digitalisation strategy aimed at promoting trust, inclusiveness, and growth in the digital economy and society, facilitating collaboration across various policy areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance digital skills and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has already taken various policy initiatives on digitalisation, including the production of a national digitalisation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to enhance the digital economy, which is crucial for future economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently embarked on the production of a national digitalisation strategy, which aims to promote trust, inclusiveness and growth in the digital economy and society.

reforms.description: The government introduced a 15% research and development tax credit to increase fiscal support for business R&D, replacing previous Growth Grants and allowing firms with tax losses to receive refunds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes R&D investment by firms, potentially leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit was introduced and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D is funded, moving from direct grants to a tax incentive model, which broadens access to funding for a wider range of firms.
reforms.source_quote: A 15% research and development tax credit was introduced in 2019, replacing Growth Grants.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government is preparing an Emissions Reduction Plan to outline policies and actions aimed at bridging the gap between current emissions trajectories and the targets required to meet 2050 objectives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to mobilize finance for low-emissions investments and support innovation, which can enhance productivity and sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently preparing an Emissions Reduction Plan for publication in 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant structural change in New Zealand's approach to emissions reduction, with broad implications for various sectors and long-term economic sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently preparing an Emissions Reduction Plan for publication in 2022 which is to outline policies and actions to help bridge the gap between the current emissions trajectory and the trajectory required to meet the 2050 targets.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has allocated NZD 600,000 in its 2019 Wellbeing Budget for digital literacy programmes aimed at seniors, helping them improve their digital communication skills and access affordable Internet.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills among seniors promotes inclusion and enables them to participate more fully in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government earmarked funds for the programmes, which have shown positive outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative addresses a significant gap in digital skills among older populations, contributing to broader digital inclusion efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The government earmarked NZD 600,000 in its 2019 Wellbeing Budget for digital literacy programmes for seniors.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has introduced a strategy for a Digital Public Service, aiming to enhance the digital transformation of public services through improved user experience and system design.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital public services enhances efficiency and responsiveness, ultimately benefiting citizens and businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched the strategy with a programme of work to improve public services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift towards a more integrated and user-focused approach in public service delivery, with potential long-term impacts on efficiency and citizen engagement.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020, the government introduced a strategy for a Digital Public Service, which set broad objectives for the digital transformation of public services.

reforms.description: The government distributed free devices to students from disadvantaged households during COVID-19 lockdowns to prevent exclusion from online education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing digital devices enhances educational access and skills development, which can improve future employment prospects and economic participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government distributed free devices during the lockdowns.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses immediate educational needs during a crisis, with potential long-term benefits for disadvantaged students.
reforms.source_quote: During the COVID-19-induced lockdowns, the government distributed free devices to students from disadvantaged households.

reforms.description: The government is considering providing subsidies for broadband access to low-income households to improve digital inclusion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Subsidizing broadband access can enhance connectivity for low-income households, improving their access to online services and job opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government could consider providing subsidies for broadband access.
reforms.importance_rationale: This proposal aims to address digital exclusion and improve access to essential services, which is significant for social equity.
reforms.source_quote: The government could consider providing subsidies for the comprehensive costs of accessing fast Internet.

reforms.description: The government has commissioned The Royal Society Te Apārangi to produce an independent academic paper on the necessary mathematics knowledge and skills learners need, aiming to improve the New Zealand Curriculum and teaching methods.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving mathematics education is expected to enhance the skill set of students, thereby increasing their future productivity and employability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has commissioned an independent academic paper by end-2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical area in education that impacts a wide range of students and has the potential to significantly improve educational outcomes in mathematics.
reforms.source_quote: The government has commissioned The Royal Society Te Apārangi to produce an independent academic paper by end-2021 on what mathematics knowledge and skills learners need to know.

reforms.description: The Industry Transformation Plan with the Digital Technologies sector has developed a new Digital Skills Plan, which includes actions to create pathways between education and work, such as digital apprenticeships and increased emphasis on internships.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of the workforce in digital technologies, the plan aims to meet industry demands and improve employment opportunities for graduates.
reforms.status_evidence: Implementation planning is underway for the Digital Skills Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan addresses the growing need for digital skills in the workforce, which is crucial for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The Industry Transformation Plan with the Digital Technologies sector has developed a new Digital Skills Plan.

reforms.description: The government plans to expand the GovTechTalent graduate programme to include all public-sector organisations, allowing IT graduates to gain work experience through internships across various agencies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of IT graduates and improving their transition into the workforce, this reform supports the development of a skilled labor force that can drive digital innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Implementation planning is underway for the expansion of the programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the GovTechTalent programme represents a significant step towards improving the digital skills pipeline in the public sector, which is crucial for enhancing overall digital capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: This programme, or similar programmes, should be opened to all public-sector organisations to enable them to engage with digital technology graduates.

reforms.description: The government is preparing legislation to establish a consumer data right, which will allow individuals and businesses to access and control their data, promoting data portability and innovation across sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates data sharing and re-use, enhancing competition and innovation in digital services.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is preparing legislation rolling out a consumer data right.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in data governance, aiming to enhance consumer rights and stimulate innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is preparing legislation rolling out a consumer data right on a sector-by-sector basis that should be presented to Parliament in 2022.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government launched the Elevate NZ Venture Fund in March 2020, aimed at increasing investment in Series A and B funding for high-growth tech businesses by allocating up to NZD 300 million into venture capital firms over five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to early-stage funding, the fund aims to support the growth of innovative startups, thereby fostering economic development and competitiveness in the digital sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was launched and is actively allocating resources to venture capital firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the venture capital landscape, addressing a critical funding gap for high-growth tech startups and enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the Elevate NZ Venture Fund (the Elevate Fund) in March 2020, a fund of funds programme that will allocate up to NZD 300 million into venture capital firms over the next five years.

reforms.description: The Digital Boost initiative was launched to provide free digital skills training and consultation to small businesses, aiming to enhance their capabilities in adopting digital technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital skills among small businesses, the initiative aims to enhance productivity and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was launched and is providing training and consultation services to small businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the digital capabilities of small businesses, which is crucial for their competitiveness and growth in a digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: As a part of the massive fiscal policy response to COVID-19, the government announced a package of NZD 20 million aimed at promoting digital capabilities in small businesses and tourism operators through training and consultation.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Elevate Fund aims to stimulate a larger supply of venture capital for high-growth tech businesses by requiring venture capital firms to raise matching capital and invest a significant portion into Series A and B funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the availability of venture capital, the fund supports the growth of innovative startups, enhancing their potential for market success and economic contribution.
reforms.status_evidence: The Elevate Fund has been established to enhance venture capital availability.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Elevate Fund represents a significant structural change in the venture capital landscape, aiming to enhance the funding ecosystem for startups, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The establishment of the Elevate Fund may stimulate a larger supply of funds in this domain.

reforms.description: The government plans to use public procurement strategically to foster the growth of New Zealand firms by promoting the introduction of novel digital solutions through ICT procurement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By leveraging public procurement to support local firms, the initiative aims to enhance the development of digital services, thereby boosting innovation and competitiveness in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to actively use its ICT procurement to promote the growth of New Zealand firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in how public procurement can be utilized to support local innovation, although it is not as transformative as the Elevate Fund.
reforms.source_quote: The government should actively use its ICT procurement to promote the growth of New Zealand firms introducing novel digital solutions.

reforms.description: The government should define agritech exports as an integral part of the agriculture exports and allocate innovation support and export promotion measures accordingly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Allocating innovation support to agritech exports is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in the agricultural sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should allocate innovation support and export promotion measures accordingly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards agritech, which is crucial for enhancing the agricultural sector's contribution to exports.
reforms.source_quote: The government should define agritech exports as an integral part of the agriculture exports and allocate innovation support and export promotion measures accordingly.
